root_text.an7.78:1.1: “Sattahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hoti.
root_text.an7.78:1.7: anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ, dvepi jātiyo …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati.
root_text.an7.78:1.8: Dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena yathākammūpage satte pajānāti.
root_text.an7.78:1.9: Āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an7.78:1.10: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu vinayadharo hotī”ti.

translation_text.an7.78:0.3: An Expert in the Monastic Law (4th)
translation_text.an7.78:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with seven qualities is an expert in the monastic law.
translation_text.an7.78:1.3: They know what is an offense.
translation_text.an7.78:1.4: They know what is not an offense.
translation_text.an7.78:1.5: They know what is a light offense.
translation_text.an7.78:1.6: They know what is a serious offense.
translation_text.an7.78:1.7: They recollect their many kinds of past lives, with features and details.
translation_text.an7.78:1.8: With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, they understand how sentient beings pass on according to their deeds.
translation_text.an7.78:1.9: They realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an7.78:1.10: A mendicant with these seven qualities is an expert in the monastic law.”
